GigWorker.title: GigWorker
GigWorker.description: Represents an individual delivery partner registered with SurakshaPay AI, enabling personalized risk assessment and policy management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GigWorker entity.

externalAuthId.description: Identifier from an external authentication system. This ensures security best practices by not storing sensitive authentication data like passwords directly. (Security Best Practice: No passwords stored here.)

firstName.description: First name of the gig worker.

lastName.description: Last name of the gig worker.

email.description: Email address of the gig worker.

phoneNumber.description: Contact phone number of the gig worker.

address.description: Primary residential or operational address of the gig worker, used for localized risk assessments.

deliveryPartnerCategory.description: The specific delivery partner category the gig worker operates in, influencing risk models and policy types.

onboardingDate.description: Timestamp when the gig worker completed their onboarding process.

isActive.description: Indicates if the gig worker's account is currently active and eligible for policy coverage.

lastActivityDate.description: Timestamp of the gig worker's last recorded activity in the system.

Location.title: Location
Location.description: Defines a specific geographical area or zone relevant for hyper-local risk assessment, disruption events, and policy coverage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Location entity.

name.description: Descriptive name of the location or geographical zone (e.g., 'South Delhi Zone A').

coordinates.description: Geographical coordinates representing the location, potentially as a GeoJSON string or delimited coordinates, defining its boundaries.

description.description: Further description of the location, its characteristics, or significance.

city.description: City where the location is situated.

state.description: State where the location is situated.

country.description: Country where the location is situated.

RiskProfile.title: RiskProfile
RiskProfile.description: Stores the dynamic, AI-generated risk assessment specific to a GigWorker, potentially linked to a particular location, used for calculating weekly premiums.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RiskProfile entity.

gigWorkerId.description: Reference to the GigWorker this risk profile belongs to. (Relationship: GigWorker 1:N RiskProfile)

locationId.description: Reference to the primary Location associated with this risk profile for hyper-local assessment. (Relationship: Location 1:N RiskProfile)

riskScore.description: A numerical score (e.g., 0-100) representing the assessed likelihood of income loss due to disruptions for the worker in the given location.

predictedDisruptionLikelihood.description: A summary or category of predicted disruptions based on AI analysis (e.g., 'High Rain Risk', 'Moderate Pollution Probability').

lastCalculatedDate.description: Timestamp when this risk profile was last calculated or updated by the AI engine.

effectiveDate.description: Timestamp from which this risk profile is considered active and used for policy pricing.

InsurancePolicy.title: InsurancePolicy
InsurancePolicy.description: Represents an active weekly insurance coverage plan for a GigWorker, detailing premium, coverage, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InsurancePolicy entity.

gigWorkerId.description: Reference to the GigWorker who owns this weekly policy. (Relationship: GigWorker 1:N InsurancePolicy)

policyStartDate.description: The start date and time of the weekly policy coverage period.

policyEndDate.description: The end date and time of the weekly policy coverage period.

premiumAmount.description: The calculated weekly premium amount required for this policy, based on AI risk assessment.

coverageAmountPerDay.description: The amount of income insured per day of disruption under this policy.

coverageAmountTotal.description: The total maximum payout amount possible for the entire weekly policy period.

status.description: Current status of the insurance policy (e.g., 'Active', 'Lapsed', 'Cancelled', 'PendingPayment').

paymentDueDate.description: The due date and time for the premium payment for this specific weekly policy.

isPaid.description: Indicates if the premium for this weekly policy has been successfully paid.

riskProfileId.description: Reference to the RiskProfile used for calculating the premium of this policy. (Relationship: RiskProfile 1:N InsurancePolicy)

coveredLocationId.description: Reference to the primary Location covered by this policy, defining the operational zone for which risk is assessed. (Relationship: Location 1:N InsurancePolicy)

PremiumPayment.title: PremiumPayment
PremiumPayment.description: Records individual payments made by a GigWorker for their weekly InsurancePolicy premiums.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PremiumPayment entity.

policyId.description: Reference to the InsurancePolicy for which this payment was made. (Relationship: InsurancePolicy 1:N PremiumPayment)

gigWorkerId.description: Reference to the GigWorker who made the payment. (Relationship: GigWorker 1:N PremiumPayment)

amountPaid.description: The actual amount paid for the premium.

paymentDate.description: Timestamp when the payment was processed by the system.

transactionId.description: Unique transaction identifier provided by the payment gateway for this payment.

status.description: Status of the premium payment transaction (e.g., 'Completed', 'Failed', 'Refunded').

DisruptionEvent.title: DisruptionEvent
DisruptionEvent.description: Records an actual external disruption detected by the system, which can trigger parametric claims.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DisruptionEvent entity.

type.description: The broad category of the disruption (e.g., 'Environmental', 'Social').

subType.description: Specific type of disruption (e.g., 'Extreme Heat', 'Heavy Rain', 'Curfew', 'Strike').

description.description: A detailed description of the disruption event, including specific conditions or reasons.

severity.description: The intensity or impact level of the disruption (e.g., 'Low', 'Medium', 'High', 'Critical').

startDate.description: The start date and time of the disruption event.

endDate.description: The estimated or actual end date and time of the disruption event.

affectedLocationIds.description: References to Locations affected by this disruption event. (Relationship: Location N:N DisruptionEvent)

source.description: The origin or source of the disruption data (e.g., 'Weather API', 'News Feed', 'Govt Alert').

isVerified.description: Indicates if the disruption event has been verified by the system or an admin, ensuring data reliability.

Claim.title: Claim
Claim.description: Represents a request for payout due to income loss, often triggered automatically by a DisruptionEvent.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Claim entity.

gigWorkerId.description: Reference to the GigWorker who filed the claim. (Relationship: GigWorker 1:N Claim)

policyId.description: Reference to the InsurancePolicy under which the claim is made. (Relationship: InsurancePolicy 1:N Claim)

disruptionEventId.description: Reference to the DisruptionEvent that triggered this claim. (Relationship: DisruptionEvent 1:N Claim)

claimDate.description: Timestamp when the claim was initiated in the system.

status.description: Current status of the claim processing (e.g., 'Initiated', 'Under Review', 'Approved', 'Rejected', 'Fraudulent', 'Paid').

claimedLostIncomeAmount.description: The initial calculated amount of income lost by the worker during the disruption, as per parametric rules.

approvedPayoutAmount.description: The final amount approved for payout after review and fraud detection.

fraudScore.description: A score indicating the likelihood of fraud detected for this claim by the AI fraud detection engine.

fraudReason.description: Detailed reason if the claim was flagged or rejected due to detected fraud.

rejectionReason.description: Reason for rejecting the claim if it was not approved.

isAutomated.description: Indicates if the claim was automatically initiated by a parametric trigger rule.

reviewerId.description: Reference to the AdminUser who manually reviewed the claim, if applicable. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N Claim)

lastUpdatedDate.description: Timestamp of the last update to the claim status or details.

PayoutTransaction.title: PayoutTransaction
PayoutTransaction.description: Records the details of an actual financial payout made to a GigWorker for an approved claim.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PayoutTransaction entity.

claimId.description: Reference to the Claim for which this payout is processed. (Relationship: Claim 1:N PayoutTransaction)

gigWorkerId.description: Reference to the GigWorker receiving the payout. (Relationship: GigWorker 1:N PayoutTransaction)

amount.description: The actual amount paid out to the gig worker.

transactionDate.description: Timestamp when the payout transaction was processed by the payment gateway.

paymentGatewayTransactionId.description: Unique transaction identifier from the payment gateway for this payout.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for the payout (e.g., 'UPI', 'Bank Transfer').

AdminUser.title: AdminUser
AdminUser.description: Represents an internal administrator or staff user within the SurakshaPay AI system, with roles defining their permissions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdminUser entity.

externalAuthId.description: Identifier from an external authentication system. (Security Best Practice: No passwords stored here.)

firstName.description: First name of the admin user.

lastName.description: Last name of the admin user.

email.description: Email address of the admin user.

role.description: Role of the admin user within the system, defining their permissions and access levels (e.g., 'Admin', 'Analyst', 'Support').

lastLoginDate.description: Timestamp of the admin user's last successful login.

ParametricTriggerRule.title: ParametricTriggerRule
ParametricTriggerRule.description: Defines the rules and conditions for automatically triggering claims based on detected DisruptionEvents.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ParametricTriggerRule entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the trigger rule (e.g., 'Heavy Rain Alert - Food Delivery').

disruptionType.description: The type of disruption this rule monitors (e.g., 'Environmental', 'Social').

disruptionSubType.description: The specific sub-type of disruption (e.g., 'Heavy Rain', 'Curfew') that activates this rule.

minSeverity.description: The minimum severity level of the disruption event required to trigger a claim (e.g., 'Medium', 'High').

durationThresholdHours.description: The minimum duration in hours a disruption must continuously last to trigger a claim.

locationTag.description: A tag or identifier for the type of location this rule applies to (e.g., 'Urban', 'Rural'), allowing for flexible targeting.

deliveryPartnerCategory.description: The specific delivery partner category this rule applies to (e.g., 'Food', 'E-commerce', 'Grocery', or 'All').

impactThresholdPercentage.description: The estimated percentage of income loss that, when met, triggers a claim for affected workers.

isActive.description: Indicates if this trigger rule is currently active and monitoring for disruption events.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the trigger rule was created.

lastModifiedAt.description: Timestamp when the trigger rule was last modified.

definition.description: Root collection for all gig worker profiles. Document ID (`{gigWorkerId}`) MUST match the user's Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`). This structure enables path-based authorization. Includes denormalized 'externalAuthId' for authorization independence.

params.name: gigWorkerId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the gig worker.

definition.description: Collection for internal administrator profiles. Document ID (`{adminUserId}`) MUST match the admin's Firebase Authentication UID. Used for Database Access Control (DBAC) roles.

params.name: adminUserId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator.

definition.description: System-wide collection for geographical locations relevant for risk assessment and policy coverage. This data is generally public/system-readable but writeable only by administrators.
System-wide collection for detected external disruption events. This data is generally public/system-readable for context but writeable only by the system or administrators.

params.name: disruptionEventId
params.description: Unique identifier for the disruption event.

definition.description: System-wide collection for parametric rules that automatically trigger claims. This collection is read/write accessible only by administrators.

params.name: ruleId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parametric trigger rule.

definition.description: Subcollection for AI-generated risk profiles, specific to each gig worker. Accessible only by the owning gig worker. Includes denormalized 'gigWorkerId' and 'locationId' for authorization independence and query efficiency.

params.name: gigWorkerId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the owning gig worker.

params.name: riskProfileId
params.description: Unique identifier for the risk profile.

definition.description: Subcollection for active weekly insurance policies, owned by a specific gig worker. Accessible only by the owning gig worker. Includes denormalized 'gigWorkerId', 'riskProfileId', and 'coveredLocationId' for authorization independence and query efficiency.

params.name: gigWorkerId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the owning gig worker.

params.name: policyId
params.description: Unique identifier for the insurance policy.

definition.description: Nested subcollection for individual premium payments associated with a specific insurance policy and gig worker. Accessible only by the owning gig worker or authorized administrators. Includes denormalized 'gigWorkerId' and 'policyId' for authorization independence and query efficiency.

params.name: gigWorkerId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the owning gig worker.

params.name: policyId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent insurance policy.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: Unique identifier for the premium payment.

definition.description: Subcollection for income loss claims, owned by a specific gig worker. Accessible by the owning gig worker and administrators. Includes denormalized 'gigWorkerId', 'policyId', 'disruptionEventId', and 'reviewerId' for authorization independence and query efficiency.

params.name: gigWorkerId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the owning gig worker.

definition.description: Nested subcollection for financial payout transactions related to an approved claim, specific to a gig worker. Accessible by the owning gig worker and administrators. Includes denormalized 'gigWorkerId' and 'claimId' for authorization independence and query efficiency.

params.name: gigWorkerId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the owning gig worker.

params.name: claimId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent claim.

params.name: payoutTransactionId
params.description: Unique identifier for the payout transaction.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-as-Authorization-Policy (QAP) principles, ensuring a robust, secure, and debuggable system for SurakshaPay AI. Each gig worker's data is predominantly isolated within their own subcollections, directly linked to their authenticated UID. 

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization Strategy):**
For all user-specific subcollections (e.g., `riskProfiles`, `insurancePolicies`, `claims`), the `gigWorkerId` is explicitly included in the document path (e.g., `/gigWorkers/{gigWorkerId}/insurancePolicies/{policyId}`). This design is critical as it eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check parent document ownership. Rules can simply compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{gigWorkerId}` wildcard in the path, making them atomic, efficient, and easier to debug. Furthermore, key identifiers like `gigWorkerId`, `policyId`, `claimId`, `locationId`, and `disruptionEventId` are denormalized and stored directly within the respective documents where they are relevant. This enables direct, efficient queries and rule evaluation without complex lookups.

**QAPs (Support for Secure List Operations):**
1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** By structuring user-owned data under `/gigWorkers/{gigWorkerId}/...`, QAPs are naturally supported. When a gig worker queries their own data (e.g., `db.collection('gigWorkers').doc(auth.uid).collection('insurancePolicies')`), the security rules can directly evaluate `request.auth.uid == gigWorkerId` (where `gigWorkerId` is the path wildcard), ensuring that users can only list their own policies, risk profiles, claims, and payments. The client-side query path implicitly provides the necessary authorization context.
2.  **Structural Segregation:** System-wide collections like `/locations`, `/disruptionEvents`, and `/parametricTriggerRules` are designed with distinct security postures (e.g., read-all for some, admin-only for others). This segregation prevents mixing data with different access requirements, simplifying rules. For instance, `disruptionEvents` can be read publicly by the system (and potentially gig workers for context), while `parametricTriggerRules` are strictly for `adminUsers`.
3.  **Admin Access:** The `/adminUsers/{adminUserId}` collection stores admin roles. Security rules can check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/adminUsers/$(request.auth.uid))` to grant broader access to certain collections (e.g., `claims`, `payoutTransactions`, `parametricTriggerRules`), allowing administrators to manage and audit system data without compromising user data isolation. For instance, an admin can perform collection group queries on `claims` and the rules, combined with the denormalized `gigWorkerId` within the `Claim` document, will allow proper filtering and access while preventing non-admins from doing the same. This also addresses the user request of easily identifying users by name for login/signin issues, as `AdminUser` and `GigWorker` profiles hold this information, queryable by authorized personnel.

Overall, this structure provides a clear separation of concerns, enforces strict ownership, minimizes rule complexity by avoiding `get()` calls, and inherently supports secure `list` operations, aligning with all core design principles and strategy mandates.
